prompt: Name one commonly-praised idea, trend, or practice in modern knowledge work that you think is overrated, and explain why.
response: One commonly-praised idea in modern knowledge work that I think is overrated is the emphasis on multitasking and being constantly "connected." Many people believe that being able to juggle multiple tasks at once and always being available make them more productive and valuable. However, research has shown that multitasking can actually decrease productivity and increase stress levels. It takes time for our brains to switch between tasks, and this constant switching can lead to errors and decreased efficiency. Additionally, being constantly connected can lead to burnout and decreased work-life balance. I believe that focused, single-tasking and setting boundaries around availability can actually lead to better results and overall well-being in the workplace.
